## [00:00 - 01:03]

This is how I think about communication programs. This is how I think about Toastmasters. It's how I think about stand-up comedy. It's how I think about theater classes. It's how I think about my online course. I think of it like a gym. There's the treadmill. There's the bench. There's the row machine. All of those different pieces of equipment represent different types of learning. If you had to ask me, Vin, which is the most important piece of gym equipment, well, there really isn't one most important. There isn't. I think it's about the combination of them that makes you great. Because if you go to the gym and then you work on your back, you work on your legs, you work on your shoulders, and you work on your whole body with all these different machines, that's how you build the most capable body, right? And the way you're going to become the most capable as an individual when it comes to your communication skills, maybe you'll consider doing my course and maybe that's the treadmill. Maybe you'll do a little bit of Toastmasters and that's the rowing machine. It's kind of like saying, what's the best book? Which one book should I read? I don't believe there's one book that is the best and the only one you should read. I think there are a series of books. That's how I kind of think about it.
